message:
perf(pager): shrink ATOMIC_PUBLISHED_MIN_SLOT_COUNT floor 4096 → 512

The per-pager direct-slot plane eagerly constructed 4,096
AtomicPublishedPageSlot entries on every Pager open, even on
single-page and empty databases. Each slot carries an `AtomicBool`,
a `Mutex<Option<PageData>>`, and — since `bd-dgpfx` — an
`AtomicUsize` back-pointer; construction pays a `pthread_mutex_init`
per slot and destruction pays a matching `pthread_mutex_destroy` via
`Arc<PublishedPagerState>::drop_slow`.

The 2026-04-23 mt_mvcc_bench 8t DWARF profile attributed ~9% of
MT self-time to this lifecycle pair (`PublishedPagerState::new`
4.65% + `drop_slow` 4.24%) on a 500-rows-per-thread workload whose
actual page working set per worker is ~10-50 pages — well below
both the old 4,096 floor and the new 512 floor. 512 still covers
every FrankenSQLite bench we ship today (mt_mvcc_bench, e2e_bench,
mixed_oltp_bench, write_throughput_bench, read_heavy_bench all stay
under 512 pages). Pages above the direct-slot capacity already
fall through to the `ConcurrentPublishedPages` DashMap overflow
plane — correctness is unchanged; hit-path cost shifts from a
direct atomic load to a sharded hash lookup for pages in the
512..db_size range on databases larger than the floor.

Opens on larger databases are unaffected: `AtomicPublishedPages::new`
clamps `initial_db_size` into `[MIN_SLOT_COUNT, MAX_SLOT_COUNT]`, so
anything ≥ 512 pages still gets an exact-sized direct plane up to
the 65,535-page hard cap.

Before: ATOMIC_PUBLISHED_MIN_SLOT_COUNT = 4_096
        PublishedPagerState::new + drop  = 14.199 µs per pair
After:  ATOMIC_PUBLISHED_MIN_SLOT_COUNT =   512
        PublishedPagerState::new + drop  =  6.063 µs per pair
Delta:  2.34× faster per lifecycle pair; ≈8× fewer pthread_mutex_t
        initializations per pager construction (4096 → 512 slots).
Memory: ~280 KB saved per fresh/small-DB pager
        (3,584 fewer slots × ~80 B each).
Bench:  rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_cc2_1777000277 \
        cargo test -p fsqlite-pager \
          test_published_pager_state_new_construction_drop_cost \
          -- --nocapture
Bead:   bd-i332r

mt_mvcc_bench 8t, 500 rows/thread, 5 iters × 7 invocations:
  floor=4096 fs_wps median = 9,391 (7,640 / 9,391 / 12,237 spread)
  floor= 512 fs_wps median = 9,386 (7,357 / 9,386 / 12,361 spread)
The lifecycle win is ~65 µs per 500 ms bench invocation — well
under the run-to-run scheduler/fsync variance — so the bench
signal sits inside noise. Production value is the per-open memory
reduction and the direct lifecycle win captured in the micro-test.

Verification:
- cargo test -p fsqlite-pager published_pages (8/8 pass; the
  existing floor-edge test uses the named constant so it picks up
  the new value automatically)
- cargo test -p fsqlite-pager atomic_published_pages (2/2 pass,
  incl. the remove O(1) vs linear-scan regression gate from bd-dgpfx)
- cargo test -p fsqlite-pager test_published_pager_state_new_construction_drop_cost
- cargo clippy -p fsqlite-pager --all-targets -- -D warnings — clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-hxhsd","title":"STUB-RSTUB-1001: [NOT_IMPL] connection.rs:17709 (SURF-SQL-CORE-001) — implement real tested perf-optimized code","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T04:09:15.609568784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:17:49.622526914Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hxhsd","depends_on_id":"bd-y5w0m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T04:09:15.888166567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3627,"issue_id":"bd-hxhsd","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Context
Stub ID: RSTUB-1001 (tracked in runtime_stub_inventory.toml)
File: crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs
Line: 17709
Feature: SURF-SQL-CORE-001
Kind: not_implemented - Runtime guard returning FrankenError::NotImplemented. Entire code path is short-circuited and no behavior executes.
Severity: high

## Code context (current stub)
```rust
    17702              return self.execute_insert_select_materialized_rows(insert, &source_rows);
    17703          }
    17704          // Chunked materialization: wrap the source SELECT with LIMIT/OFFSET
    17705          // and process in bounded batches to avoid OOM on large result sets.
    17706          // Safe here because the source and destination tables are disjoint.
    17707          let mut total_changes = 0usize;
    17708          let mut offset: i64 = 0;
>>> 17709          loop {
    17710              let mut chunked_select = select_stmt.clone();
    17711              chunked_select.limit = Some(fsqlite_ast::LimitClause {
    17712                  limit: fsqlite_ast::Expr::Literal(
    17713                      fsqlite_ast::Literal::Integer(Self::INSERT_SELECT_CHUNK_SIZE),
    17714                      fsqlite_ast::Span::ZERO,
    17715                  ),
    17716                  offset: if offset > 0 {
    17717                      Some(fsqlite_ast::Expr::Literal(
```

## Target: real implementation
Feature scope: SQL core - UPSERT ON CONFLICT, RETURNING, PRAGMA coverage, trigger variants, ALTER TABLE, window-function edge cases, or other SQL surface covered by conformance suite

Replace this stub with a correct, performance-optimized implementation that:
1. Matches SQLite reference semantics for the target feature (run against rusqlite oracle).
2. Respects the project's concurrent-mode-default=true invariant - do NOT serialize on file/page locks beyond what MVCC requires.
3. Uses asupersync primitives if async, never tokio.
4. Hot-path considerations: avoid heap allocation in the per-row loop, prefer SmallVec, inline-cache where identical patterns repeat, honor `hot_path_profile_enabled()` gate.
5. Passes `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` at workspace level.

## Test coverage required
1. **Unit tests**: inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` - happy path, boundary (empty/max/NULL), error cases.
2. **Conformance oracle tests**: 5-10 tests in tests/conformance_oracle_*.rs comparing output vs rusqlite (C SQLite reference) on realistic query shapes.
3. **Property test** (when applicable): proptest for invariants under random valid inputs.
4. **E2E with structured logging**: when crossing subsystem boundaries (parser->planner->vdbe->storage), integration test in tests/ with tracing::debug! spans documenting inputs, intermediate states, outcome. Include trace_id, run_id, outcome.
5. **Regression coverage**: existing conformance oracle suites (s55, ext, s60-s84) MUST stay green.

## Performance guidance
- W5-6 hotspot table (bd-m4s2c/closed) identifies hot paths; use the profile if this stub is on one.
- PGO pipeline landed (W5-8 bd-njyvy/closed); PGO-instrumented rebuilds may amplify wins.
- SIMD integer-batch encode landed in W6-2 (bd-1sj4b/closed); similar column-dispatch patterns may apply.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Stub replaced with real implementation; `cargo check -p <crate>` clean
- [ ] Unit tests added and passing
- [ ] Conformance oracle tests added; rusqlite oracle matches
- [ ] E2E with logging added if boundary-crossing
- [ ] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean
- [ ] `cargo fmt --check` clean
- [ ] Conformance oracle regression: s55, ext, s60-s84 still green
- [ ] runtime_stub_inventory.toml updated: mark this stub_id as resolved with date + committer

## Reference exemplars
- RSTUB-0010 (ATTACH/DETACH, resolved 2026-03-06 by BrightForge)
- RSTUB-0013 (REPLACE on non-IPK UNIQUE, resolved 2026-03-06 by BrightForge)

 {"id":"bd-i0m5","title":"§4.19.6 Networking Stack (Asupersync net): TLS + HTTP/2 Limits + VirtualTcp (Lab)","description":"Implement §4.19.6 networking stack requirements for remote effects: cancel-safe transport, TLS-by-default, strict HTTP/2 hard limits, message size caps, and deterministic VirtualTcp + fault injection harness for lab/DPOR.

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS
- test_tls_by_default: Create remote transport with default config; verify TLS is enabled via rustls; verify plaintext requires explicit configuration knob
- test_plaintext_requires_explicit_opt_in: Attempt plaintext transport without explicit config flag; verify it is rejected
- test_http2_max_concurrent_streams: Open HTTP/2 connection; attempt to create 257 concurrent streams (limit=256); verify 257th is rejected
- test_http2_max_header_list_size: Send request with headers exceeding 64KiB; verify rejection at the header size limit
- test_http2_continuation_timeout: Start a HEADERS frame, delay CONTINUATION beyond 5000ms; verify connection is reset/rejected
- test_message_size_cap_enforced: Send remote RPC message exceeding 4MiB default cap; verify deterministic rejection
- test_handshake_timeout_bounded: Configure 500ms handshake timeout; simulate slow TLS handshake (1000ms); verify connection times out within budget
- test_virtual_tcp_deterministic: Run same replication scenario twice under LabRuntime with VirtualTcp and same seed; verify identical network behavior (byte-for-byte trace match)
- test_virtual_tcp_fault_injection: Configure VirtualTcp with drop/reorder/corrupt shim derived from lab seed; verify packet drops and reorders are reproducible and trace-visible

## E2E TEST
test_e2e_networking_stack_replication_under_loss.rs: Set up two database replicas connected via VirtualTcp under LabRuntime; configure 10% packet loss and 5% reorder rate; stream 100 committed symbols from primary to replica; verify all symbols are eventually received (retries succeed), replication converges to identical state, and the entire trace is deterministically reproducible from the lab seed.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] TLS is the default for all remote transports; plaintext requires explicit opt-in configuration
- [ ] HTTP/2 hard limits enforced: max_concurrent_streams=256, max_header_list_size=64KiB, continuation_timeout=5s, fragment cap=256KiB
- [ ] Remote RPC message size cap of 4MiB is enforced (configurable)
- [ ] VirtualTcp produces deterministic network behavior for a given lab seed (reproducible traces)
- [ ] Fault injection shim (drop/reorder/corrupt) is seed-derived and trace-visible for DPOR exploration","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:37:25.975298656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T19:08:43.188838379Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T19:08:43.188811990Z","close_reason":"Completed: required transport limit tests + harness/E2E checks pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i0m5","depends_on_id":"bd-3go","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-i0m5","depends_on_id":"bd-3go.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1771,"issue_id":"bd-i0m5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# §4.19.2 Networking Stack: TLS + HTTP/2 Limits + VirtualTcp for Lab

## Spec Reference
COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENSQLITE_V1.md lines 5495–5527 (§4.19.6)

## Overview
When remote effects are enabled, FrankenSQLite MUST use asupersync's cancel-safe
network stack (TCP + TLS + HTTP/2) so that cancellation is not a "drop the future"
footgun, deadlines/budgets bound network I/O, and transport behavior is deterministic
in lab mode.

## Production Transport Requirements

### TLS by Default (Required)
- Remote effects over the network SHOULD use TLS via **rustls** (NOT openssl).
- Plaintext transport is permitted ONLY when explicitly configured for local development.
- Plaintext MUST be gated by an explicit capability/config knob (not silent fallback).

### Handshake + Protocol Timeouts (Required)
- Remote handshakes MUST be budgeted and time-bounded (deadline or explicit timeouts).
- Protocol parsing MUST also be time-bounded.
- No unbounded blocking on handshake or protocol negotiation.

### HTTP/2 Hard Limits (Normative, if HTTP/2 is used)
| Parameter                  | Value     | Notes                              |
|----------------------------|----------:|-----------------------------------:|
| max_concurrent_streams     | 256       | default                            |
| max_header_list_size       | 65536     | 64 KiB                            |
| continuation_timeout_ms    | 5000      | 5 seconds                         |
| header_fragment_cap        | 262144    | 256 KiB absolute cap              |

These prevent stream exhaustion and header-compression bombs from turning tiered
storage into a DoS vector.

### Message Size Caps (Required)
- Any remote RPC framing MUST enforce max send/recv sizes.
- Default: 4 MiB (4,194,304 bytes).
- Larger messages MUST be rejected deterministically.

## Deterministic Network Testing Requirements

### VirtualTcp (Required for Lab)
- In lab tests, remote transport MUST be swappable to `VirtualTcp`.
- VirtualTcp is in-memory, deterministic, no kernel sockets.
- Required to make replication and tiered-storage behaviors reproducible.
- DPOR-explorable under LabRuntime.

### Drop/Reorder/Corrupt Network Shim (Required for Lab)
- Harness MUST provide a "drop/reorder/corrupt" virtual network shim.
- Simulates lossy replication while preserving deterministic replay.
- Loss patterns derive from the lab seed and are trace-visible.

## Unit Test Specifications

### T1: tls_by_default
Create remote transport with default config. Verify TLS (rustls) is enabled, not plaintext.

### T2: plaintext_requires_explicit_config
Attempt plaintext transport without explicit config knob. Verify refusal.
Then set explicit plaintext knob and verify it works.

### T3: handshake_timeout_enforced
Set handshake timeout to 10ms. Connect to a non-responding mock endpoint.
Verify timeout fires within bounded time.

### T4: http2_max_concurrent_streams_enforced
Configure HTTP/2 with max_concurrent_streams=256. Attempt 300 concurrent streams.
Verify excess streams are rejected/queued.

### T5: http2_header_size_limit
Send request with headers exceeding 64 KiB. Verify rejection per max_header_list_size.

### T6: message_size_cap_enforced
Send RPC message exceeding 4 MiB. Verify deterministic rejection.

### T7: virtual_tcp_deterministic
Run same network scenario twice with same seed under LabRuntime + VirtualTcp.
Verify identical byte sequences and timing.

### T8: drop_reorder_corrupt_shim
Configure virtual network shim with known seed. Inject drops/reorders/corruptions.
Verify patterns are deterministic across replays and trace-visible.

### T9: continuation_timeout_enforced
Send HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame sequence and delay beyond 5s.
Verify connection is terminated per continuation_timeout_ms.

## Dependencies
- §4.19 (remote effects parent), §4.17.1 (PRAGMA config), LabRuntime (§4.1),
  asupersync net stack, rustls
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:37:32Z"},{"id":1772,"issue_id":"bd-i0m5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements for §4.19.2 Networking Stack

### Unit Tests (fsqlite-remote or fsqlite-net crate)

**TLS requirements:**
1. **test_tls_default_enabled**: Remote effects over network use TLS via rustls by default.
2. **test_tls_not_openssl**: Verify rustls is used, NOT openssl.
3. **test_plaintext_requires_explicit_config**: Plaintext transport only when explicitly configured. No silent fallback.
4. **test_plaintext_gated_by_capability**: Plaintext must be gated by explicit capability/config knob.

**Timeouts:**
5. **test_handshake_timeout_bounded**: TLS handshake has explicit deadline/timeout. No unbounded blocking.
6. **test_protocol_parsing_timeout_bounded**: HTTP/2 protocol parsing has explicit timeout.
7. **test_no_unbounded_handshake**: If remote host is unresponsive, handshake times out within budget.

**HTTP/2 hard limits:**
8. **test_max_concurrent_streams_256**: HTTP/2 max_concurrent_streams defaults to 256.
9. **test_max_header_list_size_64k**: max_header_list_size = 65536 (64 KiB).
10. **test_continuation_timeout_5s**: continuation_timeout_ms = 5000 (5 seconds).
11. **test_header_fragment_cap_256k**: header_fragment_cap = 262144 (256 KiB absolute cap).
12. **test_stream_exhaustion_prevention**: Verify limits prevent stream exhaustion attacks.
13. **test_header_bomb_prevention**: Verify header_fragment_cap prevents header-compression bombs.

**Message size caps:**
14. **test_max_message_size_4mb**: Remote RPC max send/recv size = 4,194,304 bytes (4 MiB).
15. **test_oversized_message_rejected**: Messages > 4 MiB are rejected deterministically.

**VirtualTcp (lab mode):**
16. **test_virtual_tcp_in_memory**: VirtualTcp is in-memory, no kernel sockets. Verify no real network calls.
17. **test_virtual_tcp_deterministic**: Same lab seed → same VirtualTcp behavior (deterministic replay).
18. **test_virtual_tcp_dpor_explorable**: VirtualTcp is DPOR-explorable under LabRuntime.
19. **test_virtual_tcp_swappable**: Remote transport can be swapped to VirtualTcp in lab tests without code changes.

**Network fault injection:**
20. **test_drop_shim**: Virtual network shim can drop packets. Verify dropped messages detected and retried.
21. **test_reorder_shim**: Virtual network shim can reorder packets. Verify protocol handles reordering.
22. **test_corrupt_shim**: Virtual network shim can corrupt data. Verify corruption detected by checksum/AEAD.
23. **test_loss_pattern_from_seed**: Loss patterns derived from lab seed and are trace-visible.

### Integration Tests
24. **test_tls_handshake_and_rpc**: Complete TLS handshake + send/receive one RPC. Verify correct data.
25. **test_virtual_tcp_replication_scenario**: Run replication protocol over VirtualTcp with fault injection. Verify correctness.

### E2E Tests
26. **test_e2e_network_partition_recovery**: Simulate network partition via VirtualTcp drop shim. Verify system recovers after partition heals.
27. **test_e2e_lossy_replication_correctness**: Tiered storage replication with 5% packet loss. Verify data integrity after convergence.

### Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: TLS handshake details, HTTP/2 stream creation/close, message size checks
- INFO: Connection establishment (TLS version, cipher), VirtualTcp mode enabled
- WARN: Handshake timeout, message size approaching limit, stream exhaustion approaching
- ERROR: TLS failure, oversized message rejection, protocol parsing timeout
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:53:24Z"},{"id":1773,"issue_id":"bd-i0m5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RoseHollow validation pass confirms bead acceptance is green in current tree: cargo test -p fsqlite-core replication_sender::tests:: (27 passed), cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_i0m5_networking_stack_compliance (5 passed), and ./e2e/bd_i0m5_networking_stack_replication_under_loss.sh (pass with deterministic marker/log assertions).","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:08:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-i28l","title":"Corpus verify: richer diagnostics + stable exit codes + remediation hints","description":"## Goal

Make `realdb-e2e corpus verify` and the corresponding tests produce actionable output when something drifts.

## Requirements

- For each golden file:
  - OK / MISSING / MISMATCH / EXTRA classification
  - sha256 expected vs actual on mismatch
  - file size and modified time in output (helps find what changed)

- Stable exit codes:
  - 0: all good
  - 1: verification failure (mismatch/missing)
  - 2: usage / config error

- Remediation hints:
  - if file is missing: suggest re-import
  - if hash mismatch: suggest recapture or investigate accidental writes

## Acceptance

- A failing verify run points directly to the culprit and next step.
- Output is parseable (JSON option) and human-readable.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance Criteria

1. Diagnostics quality:
   - Verify output classifies each golden fixture as: OK / MISSING / MISMATCH / EXTRA.
   - On mismatch, include expected vs actual sha256, file size, and modified time.

2. Stable exit codes:
   - 0: all good
   - 1: verification failure (mismatch/missing)
   - 2: usage/config error

3. JSON output:
   - `--json` mode emits a machine-readable list of fixture checks suitable for CI parsing.

4. Remediation hints:
   - Missing fixture: suggest re-import.
   - Hash mismatch: suggest investigating accidental writes or recapturing under a new filename.

5. Tests:
   - Tests cover: missing file, mismatch, extra file, and all-ok.","notes":"Notes

- This bead is a guardrail: it prevents the harness from silently running against a drifted/corrupted corpus.
- Output should be readable by humans *and* stable enough for scripts.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:25:49.503308551Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:05:17.993301379Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:05:17.993279628Z","close_reason":"Verify now ignores dotfiles and sqlite sidecars in golden/ while still flagging stray DB files; added test to lock behavior; exit codes + JSON/human output remain stable","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corpus","e2e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i28l","depends_on_id":"bd-2als.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-i28l","depends_on_id":"bd-5ic3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-i3v1","title":"Recovery demo: WAL corruption during transaction (WAL-FEC self-healing)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:20:56.962936794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:53:29.989136689Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:53:29.989110961Z","close_reason":"Implemented WAL corruption recovery demo + tests: crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/recovery_wal_corruption.rs creates a WAL-mode SQLite DB, generates .wal-fec group repair symbols, corrupts WAL frames, shows sqlite3 behavior, and recovers via recover_wal_fec_group_with_decoder. Includes intact fast-path and salt-mismatch rejection tests. All workspace tests + clippy pedantic/nursery are green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i3v1","depends_on_id":"bd-2e3s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-i3v1","depends_on_id":"bd-2r4z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1774,"issue_id":"bd-i3v1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria

","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:40Z"},{"id":1775,"issue_id":"bd-i3v1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Task: Recovery demo — WAL corruption during transaction (WAL-FEC self-healing)

### What
Demonstrate WAL-FEC: corrupt WAL frames from an in-flight transaction, FrankenSQLite recovers them using .wal-fec repair symbols.

### Test Flow
1. Start with golden copy
2. Open database, begin a large transaction (insert 1000 rows)
3. COMMIT (creates WAL frames)
4. Generate .wal-fec sidecar from the WAL
5. Corrupt specific WAL frames (e.g., frames 5-8 of the commit group)
6. **C SQLite attempt**: Reopen database. WAL replay fails or partially succeeds. Some committed rows are lost. Database in inconsistent state.
7. **FrankenSQLite attempt**: Detects frame corruption via per-frame checksums → consults .wal-fec → RaptorQ decode recovers corrupted frames → WAL replay succeeds → all 1000 rows present.
8. Verify all rows present in recovered database

### Why WAL Corruption Is Especially Dangerous
WAL corruption is the worst-case scenario for C SQLite:
- If corruption happens before checkpoint, the corrupted frames contain committed data that hasn't been written to the main database yet
- Checkpointing corrupted WAL can SPREAD corruption to the main database
- Without FEC, the only option is to discard the corrupted frames (data loss)

### Simulating Power Failure
This demo also simulates a common real-world scenario: power loss during WAL checkpoint. The WAL is partially written, some frames are torn:
```
CorruptionPattern::WalFrameCorrupt {
    frame_numbers: vec![5, 6, 7, 8],
    seed: 42,
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- WAL frames successfully corrupted
- C SQLite loses committed data from corrupted frames
- FrankenSQLite recovers all committed data via WAL-FEC
- Recovered database contains all 1000 rows
- Row-level verification against expected data
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:23:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-i61ji","title":"W5: Collapse residual prepared-DML dispatch ceremony on reusable lanes","description":"Supportive dispatch cleanup for reusable prepared DML lanes only; this is not the primary spear tip for closing the gap.","acceptance_criteria":"No unsafe skipped checks; reusable-lane wrapper time improves measurably.","notes":"Audit complete. Current dispatch path already optimized: (1) execute_prepared_with_params_after_background_status separates background check from dispatch, (2) precompiled_dml fast path uses FSQLITE_FAST_PATH_EXECUTIONS counter, (3) schema validation prebinds publication only when needed. Further optimization would require caching dispatch decisions across statement executions, which risks correctness if schema/background state changes aren't properly tracked. The W1 function-cache preservation and existing Arc pointer comparisons in apply_reusable_table_execution_state already minimize the overhead. Recommend: monitor with existing counters before adding more complexity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T14:36:14.845964125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-15T01:11:56.469710982Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T01:11:56.465214596Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i61ji","depends_on_id":"bd-c0bsc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T14:36:15.199988637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3471,"issue_id":"bd-i61ji","author":"ubuntu","text":"## What
This is supportive dispatch cleanup for prepared DML paths that legitimately remain on the reusable execution lane. It is not the primary spear tip for closing the gap.

## Scope
- collapse redundant prepared-DML dispatch checks on reusable lanes
- share the cleanup across complex INSERT and the existing prepared UPDATE/DELETE fast path
- keep the direct simple INSERT lane separate so this bead does not become another source of truth for lane selection
","created_at":"2026-04-06T16:04:03Z"},{"id":3472,"issue_id":"bd-i61ji","author":"ubuntu","text":"- Dispatch cleanup is limited to reusable-lane paths.
- No background-status, schema-change, or change-tracking checks are skipped unsafely.

 {"id":"bd-i9sov","title":"Track S: Union-Style Register File — Eliminate Arc Refcounting from Hot Path (C SQLite Mem Pattern)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:32:13.707537616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T02:36:57.955180477Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-i9sov","depends_on_id":"bd-abgqx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:32:16.445835864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-i9sov","depends_on_id":"bd-b3yw2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:32:16.044837916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3439,"issue_id":"bd-i9sov","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Problem (from C SQLite analysis: vdbeaux.c Mem struct)
C SQLite stores register values as a C union: i64, double, or char* pointer with manual lifecycle. Zero atomic operations. Cost: 0 cycles per value move.

FrankenSQLite uses SqliteValue enum with Arc<> for Text/Blob. Every clone = atomic fetch_add (5ns). Every drop = atomic fetch_sub (5ns). For a 5-column row: 10+ atomic operations = 50+ ns pure refcounting overhead.

## Solution: Epoch-scoped borrowed values in the register file
Instead of SqliteValue owning its data via Arc, make register values BORROW from the source (page buffer, literal pool, or scratch arena). Only upgrade to owned when the value must outlive the current statement.

Pattern: `RegisterValue` = union of { i64, f64, &[u8] borrowed from page, SmallText inline }. No Arc at all in the hot path. Values are valid for the duration of one opcode's execution (like C SQLite's Mem).

## C SQLite Reference: vdbeaux.c Mem struct
- Mem.u.i (integer), Mem.u.r (float), Mem.u.s.z (string pointer)
- Mem.zMalloc: reusable heap buffer for strings that need to persist
- NO reference counting — Mem owns its buffer directly

## Expected Impact: -50 cycles per row (eliminate 10+ atomic ops)
For 10K rows: saves ~0.5 ms

## Risk: Major refactor — SqliteValue is used throughout the codebase
Mitigation: Introduce RegisterValue ALONGSIDE SqliteValue. Convert only in the VDBE hot path. External API continues to use SqliteValue (with Arc for safety).
","created_at":"2026-04-01T18:32:14Z"}]}
